followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I modestly raise the overall assessment because the other reviews correctly highlighted stronger local support for the depth/SNR and high-ell TE/EE sensitivity claims in Section IV.5 and Fig. 11. I also add concrete caveats I under-emphasized before: formal TT null-test failures and the retained Polspice transfer-function error. I do not adopt the most optimistic review because it treats truncated cosmological and DESI-tension claims as fully verified.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: Gemini's 'well_supported' ratings for the SPT+ACT Planck-precision and DESI-tension claims are too strong for the supplied truncated bundle; the required Section VII details and numerical tables are not locally auditable.
Gemini's absence of human spot-check requests is not credible given the missing cosmology sections, stripped numerical values, and post-unblinding changes.

Gemini's exact multipole ranges conflict with Claude's reported ranges and with the supplied text where the ranges are stripped, so those numbers should not be accepted without checking the original PDF/source.

Claude's Gaussian-likelihood low-ell concern is weaker than the main objections because the analysis cuts to ell roughly above 300 and uses binned band powers with many modes; it is an assumption to note, not a strong objection.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: Claude's objection about post-unblinding quadrupolar leakage and polarized-beam modeling is valid and grounded in Sections IV.2.2, IV.2.3, and V.2.
Claude's covariance-regularization concern is valid: Section IV.6 explicitly says conditional tests fail before adding a sample-variance diagonal term.
Claude's note about formal TT null-test failures in Section III.5 is a real caveat, though the paper argues their cosmological impact is small.

Claude and Gemini are right that independent-frequency inpainting is an approximation that induces cross-frequency biases; I rate it minor because the paper estimates and subtracts residual templates.
The other reviews fairly emphasize that this is a major observational dataset with genuine methodological contributions, not a routine incremental analysis.

main_claims.claim: SPT-3G D1 delivers new TT/TE/EE band powers from 2019-2020 observations of roughly 4% of the sky, using very deep high-resolution temperature and polarization maps.
main_claims.notes: The observation strategy, bundle construction, band-power pipeline, and noise spectra are described in detail. Some exact numeric values are missing in the cleaned HTML bundle, but the qualitative claim is locally well supported.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract; Sections II-III; Section IV.4; Section IV.5

main_claims.claim: The SPT-3G D1 TE and EE spectra are among the most precise to date over stated high-multipole ranges and are competitive with or better than ACT DR6/Planck in those regimes.
main_claims.notes: The paper explicitly discusses SNR comparisons and high-ell sensitivity. However, the exact multipole ranges are stripped from the supplied bundle, and other reviewers gave inconsistent ranges, so the headline 'most precise' wording still needs source-level spot-checking.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract; Section II; Fig. 1 discussion; Section IV.6 and Fig. 11 discussion

main_claims.claim: The analysis uses several methodological advances: curved-sky HEALPix/Polspice processing, Quickmock/Fullmock simulation calibration, constrained inpainting for primary CMB point-source holes, semi-analytic covariance modeling, and a candl/SPT-lite likelihood.
main_claims.notes: These components are described concretely. Their detailed validation is only partly visible in the truncated bundle, but the existence and role of the methods are clear.
main_claims.paper_location: Introduction; Sections III.6, IV.1-IV.6, V.3, V.4

main_claims.claim: SPT-3G D1 is internally consistent with LCDM and with Planck/ACT DR6 cosmological constraints.
main_claims.notes: The validation program and likelihood construction support the claim directionally. The final numerical constraints and many validation details are in truncated sections or tables with missing values, preventing independent audit here.
main_claims.paper_location: Section II claims 1-2; Sections VI-VII referenced; Table 1 and Fig. 2 referenced

main_claims.claim: Ground-based SPT+ACT CMB data reach Planck-like constraining power on some parameters, and CMB+DESI combinations show 2-3 sigma shifts in some extended cosmological models.
main_claims.notes: This is plausible and scientifically important, but it depends on full parameter tables, dataset definitions, priors, and tension metrics that are not fully auditable from the supplied text.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract; Introduction; Section II claims 3, 6, 7; Sections VII.3-VII.4 referenced

main_claims.claim: The data products and likelihood code are publicly released.
main_claims.notes: The excerpt gives SPT and GitHub release locations for data/likelihood products.
main_claims.paper_location: Introduction; Section V.4; Section X referenced

missing_assumptions.assumption: The post-unblinding corrections were prompted by identifiable instrumental effects rather than tuned to final cosmological outcomes, and Appendix B fully documents their impact.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Introduction; Sections IV.2.2, IV.2.3, V.2; Appendix B referenced
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The blinding protocol remains credible only if the decision record and parameter shifts show that the additions were necessary systematics corrections and not result-driven tuning.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Quickmock simulations, after Fullmock correction, accurately reproduce filtering, scanning, masking, noise, and map-making artifacts at the level needed for transfer functions and covariance calibration.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Sections III.6, IV.1.2, IV.6; Appendix A referenced
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The analysis relies heavily on simulation-calibrated corrections. Small mismatches could bias high-precision band powers or understate covariance.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The foreground model is flexible enough within the adopted multipole cuts and does not absorb beam, calibration, or cosmological signals.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: High-ell TT is foreground sensitive, and some polarization/instrument parameters are inferred from internal frequency consistency; degeneracies could affect LCDM and extension-model constraints.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The covariance regularization preserves parameter coverage, including for extended models, not merely acceptable chi-square behavior in LCDM-like tests.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Section IV.6; Section VII and appendices referenced
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Adding sample-variance diagonal power changes the effective weighting of internal consistency information. The claim that cosmology is unaffected should be checked beyond the baseline model.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The Gaussian band-power likelihood approximation is adequate for the adopted ell cuts and binning.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: This is probably reasonable because the analysis starts around ell 300 with broad bins and many modes, but it is still an assumption behind posterior calibration for a 4% sky patch.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The SPT-lite compression preserves relevant information for any extended-model analyses that use it, not only for baseline LCDM.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The lite likelihood is validated in the visible excerpt mainly by comparison to the multi-frequency likelihood in LCDM. Extension-model use may be more sensitive to nuisance compression losses.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Do Appendix B and the full blinding record quantitatively justify the post-unblinding quadrupolar leakage correction, polarized-beam model, and foreground updates?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The excerpt says these changes occurred but the detailed decision record and parameter shifts are in truncated material.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Do Appendix A, Appendix C, and the full Section VI validation show correct coverage for the transfer-function, inpainting, covariance, and covariance-regularization approximations?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The supplied text describes the methods and some outcomes but omits much of the quantitative validation.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are the SPT+ACT versus Planck constraining-power claims correct under matched priors, lensing inclusions, and parameter definitions?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The relevant numerical tables and dataset-comparison details are missing or stripped in the provided bundle.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Is the reported CMB-DESI DR2 tension and the claimed relaxation in extended models computed with an appropriate metric and robust to model/prior choices?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: Section VII.3-VII.4 are not included in reviewable detail, and tension metrics in cosmology are sensitive to dimensionality and priors.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: What are the exact multipole ranges and numerical values for the 'most precise to date,' H0, S8, ns, and related headline claims?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The cleaned HTML bundle strips many mathematical expressions, table entries, and ranges; other reviewers reported inconsistent ranges.

novelty_assessment: The novelty is high for an experimental cosmology paper: new deep SPT-3G TT/TE/EE data, high-precision small-scale polarization band powers, primary-CMB use of constrained inpainting, a curved-sky SPT pipeline, fast simulation calibration, and public multi-frequency/lite likelihoods. It is not a mathematical proof paper; the contribution is data, instrumentation, and statistical methodology. I now rate the significance slightly higher than in my first review, while keeping technical-soundness caveats because several robustness claims remain unverified in the truncated bundle.
score_rationale: This appears to be a strong, field-relevant observational CMB paper with substantial methodological work and likely high reuse value. I raise the score slightly from cycle 1 because the other reviews identify concrete local support for the depth and SNR claims. I do not move to a 9-level strong-select because the full cosmological analysis is not auditable from the truncated bundle, and several real precision-analysis caveats remain: post-unblinding beam/leakage corrections, covariance regularization after conditional-test failures, formal TT null failures, and simulation-calibrated filtering/inpainting approximations.
summary: The paper presents SPT-3G D1 TT/TE/EE CMB power spectra from 2019-2020 Main-field observations, a curved-sky power-spectrum and likelihood pipeline, systematic corrections, simulation validation, and cosmological constraints with SPT alone and in combination with ACT, Planck, and DESI. After reading the other reviews, I view the work as somewhat stronger in significance than in my first-cycle review, especially because Section IV.5 and the Fig. 11 discussion give concrete local support for the depth and high-ell TE/EE sensitivity claims. I still would not certify the full robustness argument from the truncated bundle: the covariance regularization, post-unblinding beam/leakage additions, filtering approximations, and full cosmology/tension calculations need human spot-checking in the untruncated paper.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper states that quadrupolar beam leakage and the baseline polarized-beam model were implemented after unblinding. The leakage is detected in map-level measurements and the polarized-beam model is preferred by internal frequency consistency; both affect TE/EE nuisance modeling.
technical_objections.objection: Non-trivial post-unblinding model additions require careful audit.
technical_objections.paper_location: Introduction; Sections IV.2.2, IV.2.3, V.2; Appendix B referenced

technical_objections.evidence: The text says conditional tests fail because the covariance is highly conditioned and that a fraction of sample variance is added to the diagonal, lowering average chi-square by about 60 points while reportedly not affecting cosmology. This is a reasonable pragmatic move, but it signals unmodeled cross-frequency or covariance imperfections at the precision level of the data.
technical_objections.objection: The covariance model needed explicit regularization after stringent conditional tests failed.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section IV.6; Eq. 38; Section VI.2.2 referenced

technical_objections.evidence: The paper assumes the transfer function and covariance rescaling adequately capture anisotropic filtering and weighting effects after Quickmock/Fullmock calibration. The excerpt describes the strategy but not enough end-to-end coverage tests to certify negligible residual mode coupling.
technical_objections.objection: Filtering, masking, weighting, Polspice regularization, and covariance effects are approximated through factorizations and one-dimensional or diagonal corrections that are simulation-calibrated rather than derived from first principles.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sections IV.1.2 and IV.6; Eqs. 9-13 and 31-38; Appendix A referenced

technical_objections.evidence: The azimuth tests at 95 and 150 GHz and the year test at 95 GHz are reported as formal failures. The authors argue the contamination is small relative to sample-variance errors and harmless for cosmology, but it remains a real residual systematic in TT.
technical_objections.objection: Several TT map-level null spectra formally fail the predetermined threshold.

technical_objections.evidence: The method uses local constraining regions, inpaints the full-depth coadd rather than all bundles jointly, and treats frequencies separately, explicitly inducing cross-frequency biases that are then estimated and subtracted using Fullmock plus noise simulations.
technical_objections.objection: The primary-CMB inpainting method introduces calibrated residual biases and covariance corrections whose systematic uncertainty is hard to audit from the excerpt.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper states the impact on best-fit parameters is below 0.1 sigma, which would make this harmless if independently verified, but it is still a quality-control caveat for a precision pipeline.
technical_objections.objection: A Polspice configuration error affected TE/EE transfer functions, and the originally calculated versions were retained for consistency.
